title: Dense Briny Chinese Tofu Shui Zhu Yu
description: A traditional Cantonese dish featuring dense and briny tofu, perfect for a brunch mood, this vegetarian-friendly recipe is a variation of the classic Shui Zhu Yu, adapted to feature tofu instead of fish.

ingredients.name: extra firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: In a large bowl, mix together salt, sugar, and 1 cup of water to create a brine.
Add the tofu cubes to the brine and let it cure for at least 30 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together Chinese fermented bean paste, soy sauce, and rice vinegar.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the cured tofu to the wok and cook until golden brown on all sides.
Add the bean paste mixture to the wok and stir to combine.
Add the remaining 1 cup of water to the wok and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in chopped scallions and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the tofu hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
